reasoning: The Lean statement formalizes exactly the claim in the informal statement. `I ⊓ J` on `Ideal R` is the set-theoretic intersection, so `x : ↑(I ⊓ J)` corresponds to "any element of I ∩ J". `Ideal.Quotient.mk (I*J) x` is the residue of x in R/IJ via the canonical ring homomorphism, and `IsNilpotent` is the standard Mathlib predicate. The use of `[CommRing R]` faithfully reflects Artin's convention (Chapter 10 develops commutative ring theory; "ring" means commutative ring), which is the standard reading of this exercise. No hypothesis is silently added or dropped, and the conclusion matches.
